aliases: Coconut Curry Noodle Soup with Prawn

english: Coconut Curry Noodle Soup with Prawn

food_subgroup: Noodle soup with seafood and coconut-based broth

ontology.foodon_gap_note: FoodOn has a general laksa entry (FOODON_03311069); no prawn-specific laksa subtype exists. Closest parent used.
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: FoodEx2 code A0CKP covers laksa as a dish category; prawn-specific variant not separately coded. Closest parent used.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: No SNOMED CT code exists for laksa or prawn laksa as a food concept. None assigned.

serving.natural_language_portion: One standard bowl of prawn laksa weighing approximately 500 g, comprising noodles, broth, prawns, tau pok, fishcake, and a hard-boiled egg.

nutrient_source.title: Laksa, coconut-based, with seafood — HPB Singapore Foodo / Energy and Nutrient Composition of Food
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: HPB Foodo reports nutrient values for coconut-milk laksa with seafood. Values adapted to represent prawn laksa as served at Qi Ji, consistent with HPB data for laksa (coconut-based, with seafood toppings). Per-100 g values derived from HPB reported per-serving data for a ~500 g bowl. Secondary cross-check performed against USDA FoodData Central composite entries for coconut curry noodle soup.

gi_source.title: Rice noodle-based coconut curry soup — GI analogous estimate based on rice noodles (GI ~53) moderated by coconut milk fat and protein load
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI measurement exists for prawn laksa in the University of Sydney GI Database. GI estimated at 49 based on: (1) rice noodles GI ~53 (University of Sydney GI Database, Atkinson et al. 2008, Am J Clin Nutr); (2) downward moderation by high fat content from coconut milk (~4.8 g fat/100 g) and protein from prawns and fishcake, consistent with published fat-and-protein GI attenuation literature. Glycemic load per 500 g serving calculated as (49 × 46 g available carbohydrate) / 100 = 22.5.

hypertension.key_factor: High sodium content (~380 mg/100 g; ~1900 mg per 500 g bowl)
hypertension.guidance: Individuals with hypertension should limit prawn laksa to occasional consumption due to its very high sodium load (~1900 mg per bowl, approaching the WHO daily limit of 2000 mg), and should request reduced-salt broth where possible.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Moderate glycemic load (~22.5 per bowl) from rice noodles, partially attenuated by coconut milk fat and seafood protein
type2_diabetes.guidance: People with Type 2 Diabetes should consume prawn laksa in moderate portions, as the rice noodle base contributes a meaningful glycemic load (~22.5 per bowl); pairing with additional protein or reducing noodle quantity can help blunt postprandial glucose response.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Elevated saturated fat from coconut milk (~3.2 g/100 g; ~16 g per bowl) and high sodium
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: Individuals with cardiovascular disease should limit prawn laksa due to its high saturated fat content from coconut milk (~16 g per bowl) and high sodium (~1900 mg per bowl), both of which are established risk factors for adverse cardiovascular outcomes.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Prawn Laksa is a quintessential Singaporean noodle soup dish consisting of springy rice noodles submerged in a rich, aromatic coconut milk-based curry broth. The broth is prepared from a spiced laksa paste — typically containing lemongrass, galangal, dried shrimp, chilli, and turmeric — simmered with coconut milk to produce a creamy, mildly spicy, and fragrant soup. Toppings include whole prawns (shrimps), tau pok (deep-fried tofu puffs that absorb the broth), sliced fishcake, and a halved hard-boiled egg. The dish is served hot in a bowl and is a staple of Singaporean hawker culture, widely available at hawker centres, food courts, and casual dining restaurants such as Qi Ji. Nutritionally, prawn laksa is energy-dense (~96 kcal/100 g; ~480 kcal per bowl) with moderate protein (~5.2 g/100 g from prawns, fishcake, and egg), significant saturated fat from coconut milk (~3.2 g/100 g), and a high sodium content (~380 mg/100 g) from the broth and shrimp paste. The glycemic index is estimated at 49 (low-moderate), moderated by fat and protein. Clinically, it is a high-sodium, high-saturated-fat dish that warrants caution for individuals managing hypertension or cardiovascular disease. The dish contains major allergens including crustaceans, fish, egg, and soy (from tau pok). It is not suitable for vegetarians, vegans, or those requiring halal-certified food without confirmation of halal status.
common_user_queries: How many calories in prawn laksa?
What is the GI of laksa?
What are the ingredients in laksa?
Is laksa high in saturated fat?
How much protein is in prawn laksa?
What noodles are used in laksa?

confidence_reason: HPB Foodo provides nutrient data for coconut-based laksa with seafood as the closest approved primary source for Singapore dishes. No exact prawn laksa entry with per-100 g breakdown was directly extracted; values are adapted from HPB per-serving data for a standard 500 g bowl. GI is an analogous estimate from University of Sydney GI Database rice noodle data. Confidence is therefore medium rather than high.
notes: 1. Cuisine field in input JSON was incorrectly listed as 'Drinks & Beverages'; corrected to 'Singaporean' based on dish identity. 2. Cuisine tags in input ('Drinks & Beverages', 'Japanese', 'Fried Chicken') are erroneous field survey platform tags and have been disregarded. 3. Halal status is not confirmed; Qi Ji is not universally halal-certified across all outlets — is_halal set to false pending confirmation. 4. vitamin_d_mcg is -1 as it is not reported in the HPB Foodo source for this dish category. 5. vitamin_e_mg is -1 as it is not reported in the HPB Foodo source for this dish category. 6. selenium_mcg is -1 as it is not reported in the HPB Foodo source for this dish category. 7. trans_fat_g is 0.0; confirmed negligible/trace in coconut-milk-based dishes per HPB data. 8. GI set to 49 as an analogous estimate from University of Sydney GI Database rice noodle reference (GI ~53), moderated downward by coconut milk fat and seafood protein content. 9. Glycemic load per 500 g serving = (49 × 46 g available carbohydrate) / 100 = 22.5. 10. FoodOn URI FOODON_03311069 and FoodEx2 code A0CKP are used as closest_parent matches; no exact prawn laksa subtype code exists in either ontology.
